title: Houston Rockets vs. Phoenix Suns Game Recap: Live Score, Stats, and Highlights - April 7, 2026
body:
In a thrilling comeback victory, the Houston Rockets stunned the Phoenix Suns 119-105 on Tuesday night, overcoming a 21-point deficit to secure their latest win. The Rockets' resounding victory marked a notable turn of events in the NBA, as they capitalized on the momentum shift in the second half to seal the win. 

Kevin Durant's 24-point performance in his return to Phoenix was undoubtedly a highlight of the game, but it was the Rockets' cohesive team effort that ultimately propelled them to victory. Despite being down by 21 points early in the game, the Rockets demonstrated remarkable resilience and determination, gradually chipping away at the deficit to take control of the game in the final quarter.

The game's pivotal moment came when the Rockets narrowed the gap to just four points midway through the third quarter. From there, they continued to mount a sustained assault on the Suns' defense, gradually increasing their lead to secure a comfortable margin in the final minutes of the game. The Suns, on the other hand, struggled to contain the Rockets' offense, particularly in the fourth quarter, where they conceded 33 points to seal their fate.

The Rockets' impressive comeback victory serves as a testament to their ability to adapt and respond to adversity. With their 119-105 win, they continue to showcase their grit and determination, qualities that have come to define their team's identity this season. The victory also marked a significant milestone for the Rockets, who have now secured several crucial wins in their bid to push for the playoffs.

The loss for the Suns, meanwhile, casts a cloud over their playoff aspirations, as they struggle to find consistency in their performances. With their defeat to the Rockets, they have now slumped to a 2-3 record in their last five games, raising concerns about their ability to secure a playoff spot. As the season enters its final stretch, the Suns will need to regroup and refocus if they are to avoid being left behind in the playoff chase.

Looking ahead, the Rockets will be eager to build on their momentum, as they face stiff competition from other playoff contenders. With their confidence boosted by their convincing comeback victory, they will be looking to capitalize on their current form to secure further wins and consolidate their position in the standings. As for the Suns, they will need to reassess their strategy and make the necessary adjustments to bounce back from their disappointing loss and stay in the hunt for a playoff spot. The outcome of their next few games will be crucial in determining their fate, and it remains to be seen whether they can regroup and respond to the challenge ahead.
